message:
chore(beads): add bd-3iae (case-insensitive label filters) and rotate history

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3dh.8","title":"Implement LabelDashboard view mode for label health analytics","description":"Go's [ key opens label dashboard showing per-label health metrics: velocity, freshness, criticality, flow balance, bottleneck density. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) Add LabelDashboard variant to ViewMode enum. (2) Compute label health via label_intel module (already exists). (3) List pane: labels sorted by health score (worst first), showing health bar and key metrics. (4) Detail pane: selected label shows full health breakdown, top issues, cross-label dependencies. (5) Add sort cycling (health/velocity/issue count). ACCEPTANCE: [ key enters label dashboard. Labels show health metrics. Detail drills into per-label data.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:59:45.428946663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T05:04:35.279076902Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T05:04:35.278646747Z","close_reason":"Added LabelDashboard ViewMode with [ key toggle. List pane shows health bars, scores, and open/closed counts per label. Detail pane shows velocity, freshness, flow, criticality breakdowns plus issue list. Uses existing label_intel module. 3 tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:04:48.093812287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3dh.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:59:45.428946663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":35,"issue_id":"bd-3dh.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LabelDashboard: per-label health view showing issue counts, completion rates, avg age, trend direction. Uses label_scope/label_context from bd-1ru.4. BLOCKED BY bd-1ru.4. Go reference: cmd/bv/tui.go labelDashboardView().","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:08:55Z"},{"id":89,"issue_id":"bd-3dh.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes: (1) at least one unit test constructing BvrApp with test data, sending the relevant key event, and asserting the resulting state change, (2) an edge case test with empty/minimal data to ensure no panic. Follow existing test patterns in tui.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dh.9","title":"Implement FlowMatrix view mode for cross-label dependency analysis","description":"Go's f key opens flow matrix showing cross-label dependency flows as a matrix/heatmap. Rows and columns are labels, cells show dependency count/strength between label pairs. Identifies label coupling patterns. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) Add FlowMatrix variant to ViewMode enum. (2) Compute cross-label flow via label_intel::compute_cross_label_flow() (already exists). (3) Render as matrix/table with label names on axes. (4) Color cells by flow intensity. (5) Detail pane shows specific dependencies between selected label pair. ACCEPTANCE: f key enters flow matrix. Matrix shows inter-label dependency counts.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:00:17.403941914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T05:46:00.393025683Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T05:46:00.392437783Z","close_reason":"Implemented FlowMatrix view mode: ] key toggle, j/k row nav, h/l col nav, matrix display with cursor highlighting, detail panel showing cross-label dependencies with issue IDs, bottleneck labels. 3 tests added, help overlay updated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:00:17.403941914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3dh.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ybj.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:04:46.397679157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"bd-3dh.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FlowMatrix: cross-label dependency heatmap showing which labels depend on which other labels (aggregated from issue-level deps). Uses KPaths from advanced insights (bd-ybj.5). BLOCKED BY bd-ybj.5. Go reference: cmd/bv/tui.go flowMatrixView().","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:08:57Z"},{"id":90,"issue_id":"bd-3dh.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes: (1) at least one unit test constructing BvrApp with test data, sending the relevant key event, and asserting the resulting state change, (2) an edge case test with empty/minimal data to ensure no panic. Follow existing test patterns in tui.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dy","title":"Async Phase 2: background metric computation for large graphs","description":"WHAT: For graphs above a configurable size threshold, compute expensive metrics (betweenness centrality, eigenvector centrality) in a background thread while returning fast metrics (PageRank, k-core, degree) immediately. WHY: Go handles this via goroutines in pkg/analysis/analysis.go — expensive metrics compute in background while the TUI shows partial results. Our Rust engine blocks on all metrics before returning anything, which makes large graphs feel sluggish. This is lower priority because most real-world beads graphs are small (<1000 nodes), but it's important for the 'wow factor' on large repos. IMPLEMENTATION: Use std::thread::spawn (not async — we don't have a runtime and don't need one for CPU-bound work). GraphAnalyzer returns AnalysisResult with Option<T> for expensive metrics. TUI polls for completion and re-renders when background metrics arrive. Config system (bd-6gv) provides the size threshold. ACCEPTANCE: Fast metrics return in <100ms regardless of graph size. Expensive metrics fill in within seconds. TUI shows progress indicator while background computation runs. No unsafe code. DEPENDS ON: bd-6gv (config system for thresholds). PARENT: bd-3in","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:03:26.474517253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:43:22.624698094Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T21:43:22.624170485Z","close_reason":"Implemented two-phase background metric computation. For graphs >200 nodes, fast O(V+E) metrics (PageRank, cycles, k-core, degree, articulation, slack, critical_path) return immediately. Expensive O(V*E) metrics (betweenness, eigenvector, HITS) compute in a background std::thread::spawn, delivered via mpsc channel, merged on completion. TUI shows 'metrics: computing...' indicator in header. New API: AnalysisConfig::fast_phase()/slow_phase(), GraphMetrics::merge_slow()/has_pending_slow_metrics(), Analyzer::new_fast()/spawn_slow_computation()/apply_slow_metrics(). 17 new tests (5 graph.rs, 8 mod.rs, 4 tui.rs). Total: 1136 tests, 0 failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dy","depends_on_id":"bd-6gv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:03:44.663067770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-3dy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN: Two-phase result: AnalysisResult{fast: FastMetrics, slow: Option<SlowMetrics>}. FastMetrics: PageRank, degree, k-core (all O(V+E) or near). SlowMetrics: betweenness (O(VE)), eigenvector (iterative). TUI renders fast immediately, shows spinner for slow, re-renders on completion via channel notification. Thread join on app exit to prevent resource leak.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:09:24Z"},{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-3dy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes inline unit tests. Config: test load/merge/default. Cache: test hit/miss/TTL/invalidation. Feedback: test round-trip/apply/corrupt-file. Async: test fast-return/eventual-complete.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:48Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3iae","title":"Make CLI and analysis label filters consistently case-insensitive","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T03:29:42.528423584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T03:34:33.369521282Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T03:34:33.369245026Z","close_reason":"Made user-facing label filters case-insensitive across capacity, priority-by-label, and label-health internals with regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3in","title":"Epic 5: Analysis Infrastructure — config, cache, feedback, async","description":"Meta-epic for analysis engine infrastructure: per-metric configuration system, computation cache layer, persistent feedback/scoring adjustments, and async background computation. These are foundational capabilities that multiple robot outputs and TUI features depend on.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:02:26.976004868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:48:28.076898713Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T21:48:28.076434093Z","close_reason":"All child beads complete: config (bd-6gv), cache (bd-4h6), feedback (bd-3ud), async (bd-3dy), tests (bd-3qa). Infrastructure layer fully implemented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3in","depends_on_id":"bd-3dy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:36:07.190177942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3in","depends_on_id":"bd-3qa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:36:07.660546725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3in","depends_on_id":"bd-3ud","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:36:06.753592852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3in","depends_on_id":"bd-4h6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:36:06.248096991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3in","depends_on_id":"bd-6gv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:36:05.804251998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"bd-3in","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CONTEXT: Foundational infrastructure that multiple features depend on. The config system (bd-6gv) is needed by robot output (bd-1ru.3) and async computation (bd-3dy). The cache (bd-4h6) is needed by WhatIf (bd-ybj.4) for fast delta computation. The feedback loop (bd-3ud) is needed by triage output (bd-1ru.9). ORDER: bd-6gv (config) first since bd-3dy and bd-1ru.3 depend on it. bd-4h6 (cache) is independent. bd-3ud depends on bd-ybj.2 (triage scoring). bd-3dy depends on bd-6gv.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:09:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kai","title":"Optimize robot-triage startup hotspots with measurement-backed changes","description":"Background:
Robot-oriented triage and planning flows are usable, but startup still spends measurable time in analyzer construction and triage computation on medium stress fixtures.

Goal:
Use measurement-backed optimization passes to reduce robot-command startup latency without changing public APIs or output semantics.","design":"Implementation expectations:
1. Use rch for all cargo-based measurement and verification commands.
2. Profile first, then change one internal lever at a time.
3. Keep or revert candidate optimizations strictly from measured evidence.
4. Preserve output equivalence for triage, plan, and priority flows and record the benchmark and profile commands used.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At least one startup or performance hotspot is improved with measured evidence.
2. Focused correctness checks pass after the final change set.
3. The bead notes summarize the commands used and the measured before and after data.","notes":"Created to track the current optimization pass requested by the user.

Completed sub-tasks:
1. Collected dense, sparse, and cyclic triage timings with criterion via rch.
2. Ran an A/B comparison for the triage lookup cache.
3. Kept the cache based on measured wins across dense, sparse, and cyclic fixtures.
4. Added a follow-up graph-metric allocation cut in compute_metrics_with_config to stop cloning blocker and dependent vectors just to read their lengths.

Measured cache A/B:
- dense/500 post-cache: [1.0317 ms 1.0591 ms 1.0781 ms]
- dense/500 baseline: [1.2785 ms 1.2933 ms 1.3093 ms]
- sparse/500 post-cache: [1.2719 ms 1.3588 ms 1.4169 ms]
- sparse/500 baseline: [1.4358 ms 1.5394 ms 1.6573 ms]
- cyclic/500 post-cache: [994.26 us 1.0147 ms 1.0431 ms]
- cyclic/500 baseline: [1.0452 ms 1.1018 ms 1.1406 ms]

Measured follow-up graph-metric cut:
- stress_fixture/analyzer_new_triage_runtime before: [2.1655 ms 2.2400 ms 2.3670 ms]
- stress_fixture/analyzer_new_triage_runtime after: [2.0976 ms 2.1893 ms 2.2583 ms]
- startup profile after the follow-up cut: load_jsonl=0.922ms, build_graph=2.5ms, triage=1.5ms, insights=0.680ms, total=5.6ms

Residual blockers:
1. Agent Mail MCP transport is intermittent; CLI registration and file reservations worked, but resource reads and message operations were unreliable.
2. `cargo test --lib triage_runtime` on the current tree hit unrelated test-target errors in src/analysis/alerts.rs (`parse_timestamp` not found).
3. One `rch` bench attempt for analyzer_new_full fell back to local execution after rsync trouble, so it was not used as evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-09T17:20:39.681673217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T23:52:03.226928536Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T23:52:03.226493741Z","close_reason":"Optimized robot-triage startup: replaced HashMap<String,Issue> with Vec<Issue>+HashMap<String,usize> in IssueGraph, eliminated issues.clone() in main.rs. Benchmarks: analyzer_new_full 2.6ms→1.9ms (~27%), analyzer_new_triage_runtime 2.4ms→1.8ms (~25%). All 1248 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","benchmark","performance","robot"]}
 {"id":"bd-3m5","title":"Robot output unit tests + e2e validation scripts","description":"WHAT: Comprehensive test coverage for all robot output changes in this epic. THREE layers: (1) Unit tests for each new field/struct — verify serialization shape, field presence, correct types, default values. (2) Conformance tests comparing Rust JSON output against Go reference fixtures for every robot mode (--robot-triage, --robot-plan, --robot-insights, --robot-next, --robot-priority). (3) E2e bash script (scripts/test_robot_output_parity.sh) that runs both bv and bvr against a shared test fixture, diffs the JSON schemas field-by-field, and logs discrepancies with context. LOGGING: Each test should print what it's checking, expected vs actual, and on failure dump the full JSON for debugging. E2e script should use set -euo pipefail, trap for cleanup, and produce a summary report at the end. ACCEPTANCE: All robot outputs have matching JSON schemas. Zero field-name or type mismatches. E2e script passes with exit 0. Conformance fixtures updated. PARENT: bd-1ru","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:25:36.467310033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:04:14.776590273Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T20:04:14.775668425Z","close_reason":"Robot output unit tests + e2e validation: 50 schema_validation tests (envelope, fields, determinism, complex fixtures), 11 e2e export tests, fixed tui.rs brace mismatch. 969 total tests, 0 failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:11.094387354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:11.635123738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:11.951541878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:12.461815380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:12.870861176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:13.326629454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:16.150207549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:16.800121142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3m5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ru.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:17.310702881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"bd-3m5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING STRATEGY: This bead covers ALL test artifacts for Epic 1 (Robot Output). Tests should be written incrementally — add unit tests for each field as it's implemented, then the conformance fixtures once all fields exist, then the e2e script as the capstone. The e2e script should be runnable standalone (scripts/test_robot_output_parity.sh) and also integrated into CI. Key insight: Go may not have ALL the fields we're adding (some may be aspirational) — the conformance tests should compare against Go where Go has the field, and validate presence/type where it doesn't. Use insta snapshot tests for JSON shape validation.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:28:11Z"}]}
